thread.title: Alabama Attorney General, and 31 other AGs, sign letter urging Congress to pass Kids Online Safety Act
thread.title_full: Alabama Attorney General, and 31 other AGs, sign letter urging Congress to pass Kids Online Safety Act

author: @WBRCnews
title: Alabama Attorney General, and 31 other AGs, sign letter urging Congress to pass Kids Online Safety Act
text:
BIRMINGHAM, Ala. ( WBRC ) - The push continues to protect your children online.
Alabama Attorney General Steve Marshall, along with 31 other state AGs, signed and sent a letter this week to Congress urging leaders to pass the Kids Online Safety Act or KOSA before the year is over.
It would establish better safeguards for minors online by enabling the strongest safety settings as the default rather than having to opt in. KOSA would also allow parents to disable product features and opt out of algorithms while improving reporting capabilities.
Marshall says reports show young people’s mental health is deteriorating because of increased online exposure causing anxiety and depression.
He says government leaders need to step up to make change for the next generation but parents have to join in on the fight.
“There’s no doubt, and I don’t think that anyone disagrees, that the research shows us that there can be detrimental effects about the use of social media for our young people and I think it’s incumbent upon parents to be proactive in what access their children have as well as the amount of time in which they’re reviewing social media,” said Marshall.
He hopes with the passing of this federal legislation, there will also be a clause included which allows state to pass greater action in protecting minors online if they see fit.
